rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF IDA MAE JONES
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 8/20/25. Examiner's Notes emailed to counsel with the following issues: 1) Proposed order has not been filed. 2) Please allege why bond has not been increased. If additional bond is needed, an ex parte petition to increase bond will need to be filed, granted, and additional bond filed prior to this petition being granted. 3) A specific amount of time to close the estate must be requested and reasons why such amount of time is needed. 4) Almost $700K is in one bank. Proof of full FDIC coverage will need to be filed prior to this petition being granted. 5) Ending balance of cash must be verified. [Local Rule 14.34D]. Please also make sure to file a verified declaration pursuant to Local Rule 14.59A certifying that the original document(s) will be available for inspection in the same way as documents are maintained under CRC 2.257. 6) The accounting shows that there was fraud, but the petition does not address this. Further, the amount unrecovered ($36,718.44) in alleged to be cash "on hand", which is incorrect. 7) Petitioner alleges that an heirship petition is currently pending, which is incorrect. Please allege who the ascertained beneficiaries are. 8) Felicia Duncan was reimbursed nearly $17K from escrow. Please allege who this is and provide an itemization.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF TINA MEI
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 8/20/25. The following issues to be cleared before 8/13/25: (1) Disclaimer is irrevocable and not an assignment, disposing of the disclaimed interests as if the disclaimant predeceased the decedent. Prob. Code sec. 282. Petitioner alleges that Ms. A. Mei has two children who are entitled to her share as a result of the disclaimer. It appears that the petition needs to be properly amended to mirror the said proposed distribution (to Ms. A. Mei's issues, considering one issue is a minor), or the petitioner needs to provide an authority under which the court can grant the proposed distribution of Ms. A. Mei's shares to the petitioner. (2) The date of birth of the minor child of Ms. A. Mei. (3) The children of the disclaimant must be properly noticed.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE DAVID M. YAJKO LIVING TRUST DATED DECEMBER TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Order Confirming Successor Trustee And Trust Assets
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 9/17/25. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) The wrong form of notice of hearing was served: DE-120 instead of DE-115. (2) Who were the settlor's parents and when did they die? Did the predeceased brother not leave issue? (3) If Ken Yajko was survived by issue, they are entitled to personal service of notice. [Prob. C. Sect. 17203(b) (4) Please file a copy of each statement immediately preceding the date of Settlor's death to show how title to the accounts was vested. (Balances in the accounts may be redacted.) After the supplement to the petition is filed, a copy of the supplement must be served on the beneficiaries and a copy of the petition and the supplement must be served on any heirs-at-law, whose right, title, or interest would be affected by this petition. Please provide courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 9/3/25. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF NANCY BOLMEIER FISHER
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 9/23/25. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) No will has been filed yet. A copy of the lost will must be filed. (2) Petitions for probate of lost wills must clearly state on their face that the will is lost and both the published and mailed notice must so state. [LRSF 14.20.] (3) Mailed notice must also have attached to it the procedures for how to make a remote appearance by Zoom and CourtCall, as well as remote public access, as set out at https://sf.courts.ca.gov/divisions/probate-court. [LRSF 14.1 C.6.] (4) The Confidential Statement of Birth Date and Driver's License Number (Judicial Council Form DE-147S) is also required, pursuant to Probate Code Section 8404(b). Form DE-147S must be filed separately and confidentially with a cover sheet. (5) Attachment 3f(3) to the petition does not appear to fully address Probate Code Section 6124: If the testator's will was last in the testator's possession, the testator was competent until death, and neither the will nor a duplicate original of the will can be found after the testator's death, it is presumed that the testator destroyed the will with intent to revoke it. This presumption is a presumption affecting the burden of producing evidence. Please provide courtesy copies of the petition and any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 9/9/25 to assist the examiner (1) in knowing that the issues have been addressed and (2) in reviewing the matter. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.
